01.name: Abadar's Ledger
01.desc: Every face weighed against its twin - the god of coin keeps no thumb on the scale.

02.name: Norgorber's Favor
02.desc: The thief-god's thumb pins the one face down; the rest scarcely dare rise.

03.name: Erastil's Humble Lot
03.desc: Old Deadeye gives the one freely, withholds the five entirely, and buries the six.

04.name: Penitent's Bones
04.desc: Sworn to humility, it lies as one or two and never reaches higher.

05.name: Nethys's Duality
05.desc: The All-Seeing Eye favors the lowest and the highest, and little between.

06.name: Desna's Lucky Star
06.desc: The Song of the Spheres lands the one or the five - the faces that always pay.

07.name: Calistria's Three Stings
07.desc: Three stings from the wasp-queen: one, three, five - never an even mercy.

08.name: Gozreh's Favoring Wind
08.desc: A fair wind nudges the one ahead; the other faces merely whisper.

09.name: Kurgess's Toss
09.desc: The Strong Man's throw crowns the humble one or the towering six.

10.name: Lamashtu's Crooked Bone
10.desc: The Mother of Monsters births only the odd - one, three, and five.

11.name: Cayden's Barstool Bones
11.desc: The Drunken Hero's dice: a one, a two, a five, and one more round.

12.name: Besmara's Pearl
12.desc: The Pirate Queen's pearl gleams on the one, the five, and the six.

13.name: Sczarni Sharper's Die
13.desc: A Varisian sharper's bone - chiefly the one, gilded now and then with five or six.

14.name: Brigh's Misfire
14.desc: A clumsy counterweight pulls toward the empty two; only the one ever redeems it.

15.name: Norgorber's Quiet Edge
15.desc: The cautious cheat: the one and the five, taken without a whisper.

16.name: Droskar's Toil
16.desc: The Dark Smith hoards sixes that, standing alone, are worth nothing - and a stray one.

17.name: Pharasma's Three Doors
17.desc: The Lady of Graves opens the third door most, the first and second at times.

18.name: Shelyn's Brush
18.desc: The Eternal Rose paints the five brightest, with the one and six in the margins.

19.name: Aroden's Relic
19.desc: The Last Azlanti's relic clings to the three, flanked by the one and the six.

20.name: Iomedae's Trine
20.desc: Three is its creed; the one and the five march close beside it.

21.name: Urgathoa's Feast
21.desc: The Pallid Princess's greasy bone slips to the six, yet the odd faces still cling.

22.name: Asmodeus's Pact
22.desc: The Prince of Darkness's face completes any pact, yet alone it honors nothing.

23.name: Irori's Theorem
23.desc: The Master's perfect figures - two, three, four - and not one of them pays.

24.name: Torag's Foundation Stone
24.desc: The Father of Creation lays the six like a cornerstone; the one he forgot.

25.name: Folgrit's Hearthstone
25.desc: Hearth-sister to the forge-stone - the same steadfast, solitary six.

26.name: Angradd's War-Die
26.desc: Third of the forge-kin; the six its war-cry, the one unknown to it.

27.name: Razmir's Crown
27.desc: The Living God's die rings royal - four, three, two - and pays a pauper's sum.

28.name: Hanspur's Wager
28.desc: The Drowned Boatman's bone: shaking hands, yet the five still surfaces oftenest.

29.name: Lantern King's Blue Jest
29.desc: The Eldest paints a merry blue two that mocks the eye and pays nothing.

30.name: Lantern King's Green Jest
30.desc: The same jest in green - drawn ever to the worthless two.

31.name: Lantern King's Red Jest
31.desc: Red now, the joke unchanged: the empty two, again and again.

32.name: Lantern King's Cruel Jest
32.desc: Cruelest of the Eldest's pranks - the scoreless two, time after time.

33.name: Groetus's Tally
33.desc: The Moon of the End counts only evens - two, four, six - a hollow sum.

34.name: Fortune's Fool
34.desc: It swears the two is certain luck; yet the two is empty as a beggar's purse.

35.name: Ghlaunder's Grime
35.desc: Filth-caked by the Gossamer King, yet the five glints through - with a clinging two.

36.name: Varisian Wanderer's Die
36.desc: A wanderer's bone of threes and twos - many leagues travelled, no coin earned.

37.name: Zon-Kuthon's Curse
37.desc: The Midnight Lord shuns the one and the six; only joyless middles remain.
